events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to produce a JSON answer for a structural bolting inspection scenario. The task: determine if the connection meets pretension requirements, citing visible evidence. The scenario: moment connection specified as pretensioned. Bolt heads show F3125 Grade A325 markings (matches drawing). Every DTI washer's silicone squirts are fully extruded at all bumps. Match marks show consistent nut rotation across connection after snug-tight. No thread run-out in shear plane at visible bolts. All good signs.

Decision: pass. Risk? Minimal risk, so low. Work stage: tested/inspected (given). Component conditions: none, so empty list. Percent complete: likely 100% for this connection. Not asked for value, so 0. Sound source not relevant. Confidence: high (evidence is clear). Event time, rate not needed. Order not needed. Workable: nothing to rework, so possibly empty list. Findings: positive findings, but they ask for "short defect or hazard finding". None. So empty. Actions: none needed. Rationale: explain that all observed indicators meet pretension requirements per RCSC. So I'll output a JSON with decision "pass", risk "low", work_stage "tested/inspected", component_conditions: [] (since none defective), percent_complete: 100, value: 0, confidence: 0.95, findings: [], actions: [], rationale: "All DTI squirts fully extruded, consistent nut rotation from match marks, correct bolt grade, and no shear plane thread run-out confirm compliant pretensioned connection.".

I'll structure accordingly.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "pass",
  "risk": "low",
  "work_stage": "tested/inspected",
  "component_conditions": [],
  "percent_complete": 100,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [],
  "actions": [],
  "rationale": "The connection meets pretensioning requirements. Bolt head markings (F3125 Grade A325) match the drawing callout. Fully extruded silicone squirts on all DTI bumps indicate correct preload was achieved per manufacturer instructions. Consistent match marks and nut rotation from snug-tight condition confirm the turn-of-nut method was properly applied. No thread run-out in the shear plane satisfies the RCSC limit on thread exclusions in shear planes. All visible evidence supports compliance with the RCSC Specification for Structural Joints Using High-Strength Bolts.",
  "references": [
    "RCSC Specification for Structural Joints Using High-Strength Bolts, Section on Pretensioned Joints",
    "ASTM F3125",
    "DTI Manufacturer Installation Instructions"
  ]
}
